source_document: The Invisible Layer: 47 Asymmetric Opportunities the Mainstream Hasn't Found Yet

Cortical Labs.claim: Cortical Labs launched CL1, the world's first commercial biological computer, in March 2025
CL1 priced at $35,000/unit, each contains 200,000 living human neurons on a CMOS chip
Neurons survive up to six months
Partnership with Singapore's DayOne Data Centers to deploy 120 CL1 units in Melbourne and up to 1,000 units in Singapore
Raised ~$11.6M, backed by Horizons Ventures (Li Ka-shing), In-Q-Tel (CIA), and Blackbird Ventures
Funding details including Fortune and Data Center Dynamics sources
March 2026 round brought in Tom Oxley (founder of Synchron) and Gobi Partners
Cortical Cloud wetware-as-a-service platform is now operational

FinalSpark.claim: FinalSpark runs the world's first online neuroplatform — 16 brain organoids with ~160,000 neurons
Can reliably store 1 bit of information per organoid
Self-funded (~CHF 1M from founders' prior exit) and seeking CHF 50M
Ten universities have access including Michigan, Exeter, and Free University of Berlin
Neurons consume less power than a handheld calculator

Koniku.claim: Builds smell cyborgs fusing biological neurons with silicon — 128 active neurons detecting volatile organic compounds
Smell cyborg detecting volatile organic compounds
$8M Airbus contract for aviation threat detection deployed at SFO, though scientific skepticism is significant

BrainChip.claim: Only publicly traded pure-play neuromorphic chip company, trading at AUD 0.145 (down 94% from 2022 high of AUD 2.34)
BrainChip historical chart showing 2022 high of AUD 2.34
AKD1500 production ramp in 2026 and Akida Pulsar mass-market neuromorphic MCU

Bolt Threads.claim: Bolt Threads is dead (website reads 'no longer operating')

Amyris.claim: Amyris filed Chapter 11 (August 2023, $1.15B debt, equity wiped)

Solugen.claim: Raised $643M at a $2B valuation, with a $213M DOE loan guarantee for a Minnesota biomanufacturing plant
Raised additional $50M in March 2026 for Canadian nitrogen fertilizer scaling
Investors include Founders Fund, Baillie Gifford, GIC, Temasek, and BlackRock

Pivot Bio.claim: Validated nitrogen-fixing microbes across 15 million acres in 34 states
Paid farmers over $13M through its N-OVATOR program

Galy.claim: Closed a $33M Series B led by Breakthrough Energy Ventures with a $50M, 10-year offtake agreement with Suzuran Medical
Sam Altman and Tim Draper on the cap table

Ginkgo Bioworks.claim: ~$6.80–9.71, ~$421–552M market cap. Revenue declining ($170M in FY2025, down 25%) but pivoting to autonomous labs with a $47M government contract
Analyst consensus is bearish (1 Buy, 3 Sell)

Twist Bioscience.claim: ~$50, ~$3B market cap. Revenue of $392M growing 17% YoY, approaching EBITDA breakeven
Revenue of $392M growing 17% YoY, approaching EBITDA breakeven
Spun off Atlas Data Storage with $155M in seed financing for DNA data storage — Twist retains a minority stake

Atlas Data Storage.claim: Atlas Data Storage (Twist Bioscience spinoff) launched with $155M seed financing — the most well-funded DNA storage company ever

Agronomics.claim: Agronomics Ltd (LSE: ANIC) — a listed fund holding shares in GALY and other cellular agriculture companies. Simplest retail exposure to lab-grown materials

ValleyDAO.claim: ValleyDAO ($GROW), ~$8M market cap, funds projects at the intersection of synthetic biology and climate
The most directly relevant DeSci token for non-therapeutic synbio investing

Starcloud.claim: Became the fastest unicorn in YC history — 17 months from the program to a $1.1B valuation after a $170M Series A led by Benchmark and EQT Ventures (March 2026)
Launched the first H100 GPU to space (Starcloud-1, November 2025), trained an LLM in orbit
Partnered with Crusoe for the first public cloud platform in space
FCC proposal for up to 88,000 satellites filed in February 2026

Varda Space Industries.claim: Returned five capsules to Earth, more than any other in-space manufacturer
Series C raised $187M (July 2025) from Founders Fund, Peter Thiel, and Khosla Ventures
Top Startups listing with Khosla Ventures
Hold an FAA Part 450 license valid through 2029

Redwire.claim: ~$9.29, ~$1.78B market cap, $335M revenue. Solar arrays, ISS hardware, drone acquisition. Analyst consensus: Strong Buy, PT $13
Redwire.source: Document reference (no specific URL cited for Redwire)

Astroscale.claim: ~¥500, ~$450M market cap, $29M revenue. Only public orbital debris removal company. Below IPO price
Astroscale.source: Document reference (no specific URL cited for Astroscale)

ispace.claim: ~¥500, ~$450M market cap, ¥4.7B revenue. Closest to lunar hardware. Two crash landings — high risk
ispace.source: Document reference (no specific URL cited for ispace)

Spacecoin.claim: DePIN LEO nanosatellite constellation for decentralized internet. CTC-0 launched December 2024; CTC-1 (three satellites) launched November 2025
~$22M market cap. Partnership with World Liberty Financial and NATO DIANA
Targets $1–2/month internet for emerging markets. ~10% of 21B token supply circulating

Unconventional AI.claim: Raised a $475M seed round at $4.5B valuation (December 2025) — one of the largest seed rounds in history
Founded by Naveen Rao (sold MosaicML to Databricks for $1.3B, sold Nervana to Intel for $400M+). Backed by a16z, Lightspeed, Sequoia, Lux Capital, and Jeff Bezos
Targeting 1,000x energy efficiency through brain-inspired analog computing. First chips expected to tape out 2026

Lightmatter.claim: Raised $850M at a $4.4B valuation, with a $400M Series D (October 2024) led by T. Rowe Price with Fidelity and GV
Lightmatter Series D details, valuation quadrupled to $4.4B
CEO stated this is 'probably our last private round' — IPO likely imminent
Deployed with 'every major data center company from Microsoft to Amazon to xAI and OpenAI'
Lightmatter's S-1 filing is the most important single signal to watch — when the $4.4B photonic computing company files for IPO

Mythic AI.claim: Came back from the dead — ran out of cash November 2022
Restructured and raised a $125M oversubscribed round (December 2025) led by DCVC with NEA, SoftBank KR, and Honda
Analog processing units claim 750x more tokens per second per watt than NVIDIA GPUs for LLMs
Honda is co-developing a 100x efficient chip for next-gen vehicles

Marvell Technology (Celestial AI).claim: Celestial AI was acquired by Marvell (NASDAQ: MRVL) for up to $5.5B (December 2025). Revenue projected to ramp late 2028, billion-dollar run rate by 2029
Marvell is now the best public photonics exposure

Rain AI.claim: Despite Sam Altman's backing, their $150M Series B collapsed in Q1 2025. The company is reportedly exploring a sale. OpenAI, NVIDIA, and Microsoft are reportedly interested in acqui-hiring

Biomemory.claim: Biomemory (Paris) acquired Catalog Technologies (March 2026), creating the largest combined DNA storage IP portfolio

Celo.claim: ~$0.07–0.09, down 99.2% from ATH ($10.66). #1 Layer 2 by daily active users (840K DAU), #1 transport layer for USDT by weekly active users (surpassing Tron), 11M MiniPay wallets via Opera browser integration
$65B+ stablecoin volume since L2 migration. Protocol revenue grew 365% in 2025
Community vote on buyback-and-burn of 50%+ of protocol profits is underway

HAQQ / Islamic Coin.claim: ~$0.02, down 96% from ATH. The first purpose-built Shariah-compliant Layer 1
1.8 billion Muslims seeking compliant products and almost zero mature crypto offerings
Has a fatwa issued, Indonesian pre-approval, and a Shariah Oracle validating every transaction
10% of all new ISLM goes to an Evergreen DAO for Muslim community projects

Huma Finance.claim: ~$0.025, ~$64M market cap. Q4 2025 delivered $1.1B in originations (+24.4% QoQ) and $2.2B transaction volume
Partnerships with Tala ($50M tokenized lending for underbanked)
Geoswift (Amazon seller settlements), and Circle CPN (29 institutions, expanding to 185 corridors)

DIMO.claim: ~$0.013, down 98% from ATH. 184,183 active connected vehicles streaming verified data for insurance, maintenance, and DeFi auto loans. Available on Coinbase

Arkreen.claim: ~$0.0003, $1.7–2.9M market cap. 300,000+ distributed solar energy data nodes connected globally, 140 GWh of green energy tokenized
Extremely illiquid — $97/day DEX volume. Available on Uniswap V3 (Polygon) and MEXC

Silencio.claim: ~$0.00005, $700K–1.3M market cap. 1.1 million users contributing noise data
Uses include urban planning and real estate analytics

eGenesis.claim: Received FDA IND clearance for Phase I/II/III study of EG-EN2784 (September 2025). Patient Tim Andrews received a pig kidney in January 2025 and has surpassed seven months post-transplant

EGEN-2784 pig has 69 gene edits: 3 xeno-antigen knockouts, 7 human transgene insertions, 59 PERV inactivations. Total funding: $454–481M from Lux Capital, ARCH Ventures, Khosla Ventures, Leaps by Bayer, DaVita

United Therapeutics.claim: ~$13B+ market cap, profitable from its pulmonary hypertension drug franchise. Revivicor subsidiary produces 10-gene-edited pigs
FDA cleared the IND for its UKidney EXPAND clinical trial (February 2025), with the first transplant performed November 2025

Building a $100M organ production facility. Seven organ programs total. Best risk-adjusted frontier play in the entire report — a profitable company with existing revenue where xenotransplantation represents massive free optionality

Life Biosciences.claim: Received the first-ever FDA IND clearance for a cellular rejuvenation therapy (January 2026). ER-100 delivers three reprogramming transcription factors via intravitreal injection for optic neuropathies
Clinical Trials Arena coverage of FDA approval
Clarivate coverage of longevity biopharma potential
Just closed $80M in financing (April 8, 2026). Co-founded by David Sinclair

American Superconductor.claim: ~$29–34, ~$1.5B market cap, profitable and growing. Revenue running at ~$280M/year with 20%+ growth and margins above 30%

VEIR.claim: Privately held, $117M raised, backed by Microsoft's Climate Innovation Fund. Demonstrated 3MW superconducting cable delivery for data centers in November 2025, with commercial launch targeted 2027
AMSC supplies HTS wire for this ecosystem

Evolv Technology.claim: Metamaterial-based security screening deployed in stadiums and hospitals. FY2025 revenue $145.9M (+40% YoY) with first-ever GAAP net income of $10.9M in Q4

Perovskite Solar (Oxford PV / First Sola.claim: Oxford PV shipped the world's first commercial perovskite-silicon tandem modules in September 2024
Licensed patents to First Solar (NASDAQ: FSLR) in February 2026
Oxford PV and Trinasolar announce a landmark perovskite PV patent licensing agreement
Hanwha Qcells achieved 28.6% tandem cell efficiency using mass-production processes, with commercial production planned 2026
Caelux shipped 'Active Glass' that converts existing silicon panels to tandem performance — potentially the fastest route to adoption

Unusual Machines.claim: Manufactures NDAA-compliant drone components — flight controllers, ESCs, motors, cameras, and FPV goggles (Fat Shark brand)
FY2025 revenue: $11.2M (+101% YoY). Q4 alone was $4.9M (+133% sequentially). $103M cash with zero debt
Pentagon's Drone Dominance program represents a $90M component opportunity in 2026 and $250M in 2027. Company describes itself as 'supply-constrained, demand exceeds supply through 2027'

Ouster.claim: LiDAR sensors for drones, robotics, and autonomous systems. Revenue $169.4M (+52.5% YoY) with 12 consecutive quarters of product revenue growth
Acquired StereoLabs (AI vision cameras) in February 2026
Analyst consensus: Strong Buy, average target $39.25 (101% upside from ~$21)

Terra Industries.claim: Formerly Terrahaptix, Abuja, Nigeria. Building Africa's biggest drone factory — 15,000 sq ft, 30,000 drones/year capacity
Raised $34M in January 2026 from 8VC and Lux Capital

Geodnet.claim: Most legitimate DePIN drone infrastructure play. Decentralized network of 20,000+ GNSS reference stations in 148 countries providing centimeter-level positioning data
80% of revenue funds token buyback-and-burn. ~$58M market cap

Niagen Bioscience (ChromaDex).claim: Only profitable public longevity company. FY2025: $129.4M net sales (+30% YoY), $17.4M net income (+103% YoY), 64.3% gross margin, $64.8M cash
Products: Tru Niagen (NR supplement) and Niagen IV (pharmaceutical-grade NAD+ therapy at 1,200+ clinics)

Rejuvenate Bio.claim: George Church's gene therapy company — has an active Wefunder campaign (Reg CF, ~$100 minimum, $59.4M valuation). Delivers longevity genes (FGF21, sTGFbR, a-Klotho) via gene therapy
Employee count dropped 67% to ~2 employees from 2023 to 2024. Extremely high risk

Loyal.claim: Closest to FDA approval of any longevity drug for any species. Two of three required FDA sections accepted for LOY-002 (senior dog lifespan extension)
STAY study: 1,300 dogs across 70 clinics, the largest veterinary trial in history. $150M+ raised from Valor, Khosla, and Collaborative Fund. Market launch potentially 2026

Blueprint (Bryan Johnson).claim: Bryan Johnson's Blueprint raised $60M from celebrity angels (Kim Kardashian, Naval Ravikant, Alex Hormozi, Winklevoss twins)
Johnson's personal Epstein correspondence revelations (February 2026) add reputational risk

Cyclic Materials.claim: Raised $162M including a $75M Series C led by T. Rowe Price (January 2026). Named MIT Tech Review Top 10 Climate Tech
Three facilities operational or under construction across Canada and the US, with VACUUMSCHMELZE's exclusive 10-year recycling partnership

MP Materials.claim: ~$50, ~$9B market cap — America's only fully integrated mine-to-magnet rare earth producer. $400M DoD investment (15% equity stake), $1.25B '10X' magnet campus announced February 2026, 100% of output committed to Pentagon

USA Rare Earth.claim: Offers earlier-stage exposure with a $3.1B government capital package

Copenhagen Atomics.claim: Most advanced Western thorium molten salt reactor developer. Two full-size non-fission prototypes built, critical experiment at Paul Scherrer Institute planned 2026–27 — the first European thorium MSR critical experiment ever. Commercial reactors targeted early 2030s. Private, €25M+ raised

Energy Recovery.claim: ~$11, desalination pure play at a 52-week low after abandoning its CO2 business and missing guidance. Secular tailwind of global water scarcity makes this a beaten-down value play

Enapter.claim: Enapter AG (Frankfurt: H2O) — the only public pure-play AEM electrolyzer company. Anion Exchange Membrane technology uses no iridium, platinum, or titanium — a massive cost advantage. German micro-cap

LanzaTech.claim: The only public pure-play carbon recycling company. LanzaJet Freedom Pines Fuels plant (Soperton, GA) began operating November 2025 — the world's first commercial-scale ethanol-to-jet sustainable aviation fuel plant
Q4 2025 licensing revenue: $16.7M (vs. $1.1M in Q4 2024). LanzaJet's separate entity was valued at $650M

Twelve.claim: Raised $728M to build its first AirPlant converting CO2 + water + renewable electricity into sustainable aviation fuel and chemicals
Backed by Amazon's Climate Pledge Fund, United Airlines Ventures, and Mitsui. First plant under construction in Moses Lake, Washington

Stardust Solutions.claim: Raised the largest-ever geoengineering round — $75M Series A (October 2025) led by Lowercarbon Capital (Chris Sacca). US-Israeli company plans stratospheric aerosol injection using proprietary reflective particles (not sulfur dioxide)
Extreme ethical and regulatory risk — banned in Mexico, multiple US states proposing bans

Nectome.claim: Brain uploading remains science fiction. Nectome is largely dormant with 4 employees and ~$120K in funding

Substrate (Founders Fund).claim: X-ray lithography challenging ASML's monopoly, $100M at $1B valuation — in Founders Fund's 2025–26 portfolio

General Matter.claim: HALEU nuclear fuel production, led by a former Founders Fund partner — in Founders Fund's 2025–26 portfolio

Rhoda AI.claim: $450M Series A for robotic intelligence — in Founders Fund's 2025–26 portfolio

Halter.claim: $220M Series E for AI cattle management — Thiel personally oversaw the round

Synchron.claim: Tom Oxley (founder of Synchron) participated in Cortical Labs' March 2026 round

Pocket (YC W2026).claim: $27M ARR at seed stage — Y Combinator W2026 standout

Voltair (YC W2026).claim: Self-charging drones that perch on power lines — Y Combinator W2026 standout

Asimov (YC W2026).claim: 5,000+ contributor motion dataset for training robots — Y Combinator W2026 standout

Human Archive (YC W2026).claim: Archiving the physical world for embodied AI — Y Combinator W2026 standout

Altos Labs.claim: Forge Global alerts for Altos Labs pre-IPO shares — mentioned as monitoring recommendation

Aspen Aerogels (avoid).claim: Revenue collapsed 40%, stock at ~$3, massive impairment charges — listed as avoid
Aspen Aerogels SEC filing on impairment

NSF Biocomputing Programs.claim: NSF has deployed $14M across seven university programs for biocomputing
Biological computing's energy advantage covered by WebProNews and Fortune

DePIN Ecosystem (General).claim: 2025 DePIN annual review — from concept validation to scaling breakthrough

DARPA Programs (General).claim: Altermagnetism for Devices — entirely new physics for computing, no commercial players exist yet
ARPA-H's AIR program — autonomous surgical robotics; Sentante already has FDA breakthrough designation
Biostasis — chemical biology to slow biological systems without cold-chain

Patent Intelligence (General).claim: Fastest-growing patent cluster globally: 'Operating or servicing of cells' (electrolysis/green hydrogen) — #1 for the second consecutive year, growing +23%. LG Energy Solution jumped 22 positions in patent rankings

Monitoring Resources (General).claim: Tracxn, PitchBook, and Crunchbase alerts for private company developments
EquityZen and Forge Global for pre-IPO share availability
Y Combinator company directory filtered by latest batch
Longevity monitoring via longevity.technology/news and ClinicalTrials.gov
Shield AI at $5.3B valuation — combat-validated AI pilot, 130+ Ukraine sorties
